text:
There once was a clumsy old cat named Sir Mittens. Every morning, he would meow at his reflection in the mirror, proclaiming, "Well, another perfect miaow-day!" Nothing could persuade him otherwise. The only thing he ever really did well was catching moths around his lamp.

One night, a particularly large moth fluttered into his lamp light. Instead of catching it with his famous mittened paws, he merely flicked it onto the floor. It rolled away.

The next day, Sir Mittens tried again. This time, just as he was about to paw it, it flew across his face. Startled, he dropped his mittens. The moth was gone.

That night, he decided to catch that moth. Late February is moth season, and Sir Mittens became a moth aficionado. He spent hours hanging traps everywhere he could think of.

The next morning, he was disappointed to discover nobody had been mothing any mouthing.

His friend Mr.
